title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: Sociologists studying digital communities have noted a shift in how people form relationships online. Early commentators (0) assumed that virtual interactions lacked depth, but recent findings challenge this view.||Many users now (1) meaningful connections through shared interests rather than geographical proximity. These communities can (2) a sense of belonging for individuals who feel isolated offline.||However, researchers warn that online spaces can also (3) echo chambers, where users only encounter opinions that (4) their own. This can (5) polarisation and reduce exposure to diverse viewpoints.||To mitigate these risks, platforms are beginning to (6) algorithms that promote varied content. Yet critics argue that such measures may (7) user autonomy. The challenge is to (8) digital environments that encourage both engagement and critical thinking.

example.explanation: “Assumed” is the correct verb for early generalisations.

questions.explanation: “Cultivate a sense of belonging” is the natural collocation.
“Foster echo chambers” is the correct metaphor.
“Tweak algorithms” is idiomatic in tech contexts.
